items.title: Premier says algal bloom 'gone' despite expansion of fishing restrictions

children.content: Fishing restrictions will be strengthened in South Australian waters because of the algal bloom's devastating impact on stocks — but the government says the bloom itself has "essentially cleared up" along most of the state's coast.

gallery.altText: Professor Mike Steer speaking to the media in the foreground with Premier Peter Malinauskas in the background
Brown foam on the shores of Glenelg.
A dead, dry fish on sand with the sea in the background

thumbnail.altText: Professor Mike Steer speaking to the media in the foreground with Premier Peter Malinauskas in the background
Brown foam on the shores of Glenelg.
A dead, dry fish on sand with the sea in the background

items.title: SA Police search waterways for knives linked to alleged murder of Caleb Leriano

children.content: SA Police will search waterways in Adelaide's south today to find three knives they believe are connected to the alleged murder of 24-year-old Caleb Leriano.

image.altText: A young man with glasse smiles at the camera in a bright room

items.title: Mother's impassioned plea after young daughter's cerebral palsy diagnosis

children.content: Ayla doesn't let disability slow her down, but her mother wants her to have the same opportunities as others.

image.altText: A girl in a blue and white dress, with pink glasses smiling while sitting in a park

items.title: Greyhound Racing SA employees fired after investigation into alleged wagering activity

children.content: Greyhound Racing SA says two of its employees from the integrity team have been stood down after an investigation into alleged wagering activity.

gallery.altText: Eight greyhounds race down a sand track at Cannington.

thumbnail.altText: Eight greyhounds race down a sand track at Cannington.

items.title: Eyre Peninsula endures 'slow' Easter school holidays as fuel costs take a toll

children.content: Fuel costs force down tourism numbers in some typically busy South Australian towns across Easter and the school holidays.

image.altText: A man standing in front of a group of caravans.

items.title: Wheelchair user calls for 'common sense' after using accessible zone without permit

children.content: Disability advocate and wheelchair user Shane Hryhorec is calling for Adelaide Airport to use a common sense approach after being threatened with a fine for using an accessible pick-up zone without a permit.

image.altText: A man in a wheelchair with his service dog on his lap

items.title: Subdivisions taking place in Adelaide's 'visionary' Hills Face Zone

children.content: The Hills Face Zone was legislated to protect the natural character of Adelaide's landscape backdrop but, despite subdivisions not being permitted, there have been 11 over the past decade.

image.altText: Face of hillside stretching into distance with some houses among trees

items.title: Police say body found on Fleurieu Peninsula was murder victim

children.content: SA Police say initial investigations suggest a person whose body has been found in bushland south of Adelaide was murdered.

image.altText: Two police officers walk on a steeply sloped dirt road among trees

items.title: This plant is the only known wild specimen in the world, found on just 300m of roadside

children.content: Only one known single spyridium plant is left in the wild, in crushed limestone on the side of a road. But scientists are hunting for more.

image.altText: The small fuzzy white flower of the Woods Well Spyridium

items.title: Dawson fights back tears after return as GWS edge Roos amid ARC drama

children.content: Adelaide skipper Jordan Dawson breaks down in tears after playing his first match since his brother's tragic death, as GWS prevails against North with the aid of a major ARC blunder.

image.altText: Jordan Dawson shares a heartfelt hug with Hugh McCluggage

items.title: 108yo and 103yo WWII veterans meet after more than 80 years

children.content: Two of Australia's oldest veterans served in the same place at the same time during World War II, and after the fighting, both headed home to resume their lives in SA — but the 108-year-old and the 103-year-old had never met until recently.

gallery.altText: Two elderly men seated at a table set for a lunch with a paper program

thumbnail.altText: Two elderly men seated at a table set for a lunch with a paper program

items.title: The donkey marching for the memory of all animals that serve in war

children.content: Penny the donkey, adorned in bright purple poppies, is spreading the Anzac Day legacy not just in Australia, but also in Cheshire in England.

image.altText: A grey donkey face up close in a paddock

items.title: Bitumen standards lowered after supply caught in Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Drivers could soon be travelling on roads built for US and European climates, as supply disruptions force governments to change the standard of bitumen used in Australia.

image.altText: Three workers in high-vis clothing fill a pothole with bitumen behind a truck.

items.title: Missing 5yo girl believed to have been abducted near Alice Springs, police say

children.content: Northern Territory Police say they believe a missing five-year-old girl in Alice Springs was abducted from her home at a town camp early on Sunday morning.

image.altText: Young girl in pink dress does peace sign with fingers

items.title: 'I know it's illegal': Artist behind 'Aussie' posters reveals new target

children.content: Peter Drew has been hanging his controversial posters around Australia for 10 years, and has no plans to stop.

image.altText: A man with dark hair and a big smile

items.title: Sabastian Sawe ran the marathon in an 'unbelievable time'. Here's why we can believe in it

children.content: Sabastian Sawe has smashed through one of athletics' unbreakable barriers by running a marathon faster than two hours. Some call it unbelievable, but the Kenyan runner has done everything he can to ensure it stands the test of time.

gallery.altText: Sabastian Sawe stands next to his record time
Sabastian Sawe holds out his hands
Sabastian Sawe runs past Big Ben

thumbnail.altText: Sabastian Sawe stands next to his record time
Sabastian Sawe holds out his hands
Sabastian Sawe runs past Big Ben

items.title: Indigenous veteran booed at Anzac Day service 'mortified' by heckling

children.content: The Whadjuk Noongar elder who was booed while giving a Welcome to Country at Perth's Anzac Day dawn service says she had a right to speak at the event and will not back down.

image.altText: An older woman, an Aboriginal elder, sits at an outdoor event wearing a dark jacket with military medals pinned to it.

items.title: ASX 200 down, oil prices up, AUD stronger — as it happened

children.content: The ASX 200 has made a disappointing start to the week, despite both the S&P 500 and tech-centric Nasdaq indexes setting new record highs, while oil prices are surging with Brent crude around $US107 a barrel.

image.altText: A stock trader looks down at a tablet as he works on the floor of a busy stock exchange while surrounded by screens.

items.title: Australia's fugitives found sanctuary in Istanbul. Then it all fell apart

children.content: Istanbul was once the hideout of choice for Australian crooks. Now a Sydney gangster's legal challenge has revealed how it all unravelled for the "Aussie Cartel".

gallery.altText: A graphic of a bearded man in grainy black-and-white against a red backdrop of the Turkish flag and Istanbul skyline.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic of a bearded man in grainy black-and-white against a red backdrop of the Turkish flag and Istanbul skyline.

items.title: 'Membership to nothing': The multi-million-dollar Wild West of rewards clubs

children.content: "Rewards clubs" have exploded in Australia, claiming to have given away hundreds of millions in prizes, but they operate with almost no legal scrutiny.

image.altText: A large 4 wheel drive in a showroom clouded in smoke with dramatic red lighting.

items.title: An oil shock, a federal budget and the recession we may have to have

children.content: Accompanying the oil shock unfolding in the Strait of Hormuz is a profound change in the way fiscal and monetary policymakers operate. And as the government prepares the budget with the impact of the Iran war still unfolding, a recession may be ahead.

image.altText: A man in a suit from a side profile with his chin jutted out defiantly.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Magnitude-6.1 earthquake and raging wildfires strike Japan's north

children.content: A preliminary magnitude-6.1 earthquake struck northern Japan early today, the US Geological Survey has reported, but there are no immediate reports of fatalities or damage.

image.altText: A firefighter in a forest in Japan spraying water onto burnt areas

items.title: 'She's like Will Ferrell': Why Kitty Flanagan said yes to Anne Edmonds

children.content: Everything Anne Edmonds has done over the last two decades has been leading to Bad Company, an unfettered satire of the theatre world the comedian created, wrote and stars in alongside Kitty Flanagan.

image.altText: Kitty Flanagan, left, crosses her arms next to Anne Edmonds, right, as both stand in a theatre.

items.title: Australian surgeon returns for fourth Gaza mission

children.content: Orthopaedic surgeon Herwig Drobetz returns to Gaza for fourth mercy mission.

gallery.altText: 29 nurses and doctors in scrubs, male and female standing, facing the camera in front of tents on sandy soil

thumbnail.altText: 29 nurses and doctors in scrubs, male and female standing, facing the camera in front of tents on sandy soil

items.title: Pogačar looks unbeatable, but Seixas could be France's next new hope

children.content: Tadej Pogačar claims a third monument win out of four for the year at Liège-Bastogne-Liège, but a new power is rising in the form of 19-year-old Paul Seixas, with an unlikely Tour de France tilt seemingly on the cards.

image.altText: Tadej Pogacar looks behind him at Paul Seixas

items.title: Trump turmoil is political kryptonite in Australia

children.content: As Donald Trump's approval in Australia sinks, Anthony Albanese needs to walk a careful line: keep voters convinced of the merits of the alliance, while avoiding the impression he is being too reverential.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese takes a selfie with a smiling Donald Trump

items.title: Farmers examine sheep for magic numbers to navigate drought

children.content: With a quick feel of a sheep's back, farmers score their flock, finding the magic numbers to help them navigate record-breaking drought, and now diesel price spikes.

image.altText: A man in a shed feels a sheep's back

items.title: Spanish derby descends into chaos after keeper punches opponent in face

children.content: The Aragonese derby between Real Zaragoza and Huesca descends into a wild brawl after Zaragoza goalkeeper Esteban Andrada punches Huesca skipper Jorge Pulido in the face.

image.altText: Esteban Andrada punches Jorge Pulido

items.title: Australia to sell fleet of Bushmaster armoured vehicles to the Dutch

children.content: Australian Bushmaster armoured vehicles are being sent to the Netherlands following a request to Australia by the Dutch government.

gallery.altText: A Bushmaster sporting Ukrainian flags stands in front of a Globemaster airplane on the tarmac

thumbnail.altText: A Bushmaster sporting Ukrainian flags stands in front of a Globemaster airplane on the tarmac
